definitions.definition: The branch of physics that deals with the relationship between heat, work, and energy.
A region of space where changes occur due to interactions with its surroundings.
The entire region outside the system.
The surface that separates the system from its surroundings.
A function whose value does not depend on the path taken to reach that value.
A function whose value depends on the path taken to reach that value.
The total energy of the system, including kinetic energy, potential energy, and potential energy due to the interactions of the particles.
The total energy of the system including internal energy and the energy associated with the pressure and volume of a system.
A measure of the disorder or randomness of a system.
A measure of the average kinetic energy of the particles in a system.
A temperature scale that has the same unit of measurement as the Celsius scale.
The energy transferred from one system to another due to a temperature difference.
The energy transferred from one system to another due to a pressure-volume change.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted from one form to another.
The total entropy of an isolated system always increases over time.
A process that occurs on its own without any external influence.
A process that does not occur on its own and requires external influence.
A state in which the rates of forward and reverse processes are equal.
A condition where the concentrations of reactants and products in a system remain constant over time.
